spec_match.rationale: The live demo is faked using JavaScript RGBA values instead of applying the actual CSS alpha() function dynamically.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive, allowing the user to change the base color and toggle light/dark modes to see the blending effect.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb and code comments accurately explain how alpha-based tints adapt to light and dark background surfaces.

edge_cases.rationale: Fakes the implementation with RGBA, thus avoiding the need to handle native CSS support detection and fallback.

references.rationale: Cites a ChromeStatus feature link in the 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system, using custom properties and maintaining good contrast.

openQuestions.title: Use native alpha() function in live preview
openQuestions.detail: The demo fakes the adaptive palette using JavaScript rgba() calculations. It should instead define a CSS custom property like --brand-color dynamically, and use native alpha(var(--brand-color), N%) in the CSS for the swatches and card elements, with a fallback mechanism for unsupported browsers.
